facebook.caption: Radisson Blu Resort, Phu Quoc sits right on a private stretch of Bai Dai, one of the longest and least crowded beaches on the island. With a 4.8/5 rating across more than 6,000 reviews, it consistently earns its reputation as one of Phu Quoc's top upmarket stays. Expect a stunning outdoor pool, a laid-back restaurant, and three bars to keep you well fed and watered. Practical tip: Bai Dai is on the northwest coast, so sunsets here are absolutely worth staying out for.
facebook.video_caption: Watch the sun drop straight into the sea from a private beach that most Phu Quoc visitors never find. This is Radisson Blu Resort, and the pool, the bars, and that shoreline are every bit as good as they look. Three bars, one dreamy outdoor pool, and a restaurant steps from the water.

instagram.caption: Private beach, three bars, and a pool that practically begs you to stay all day. Radisson Blu Resort, Phu Quoc rates 4.8/5 for a reason. Bai Dai sunsets alone are worth the trip out here.
instagram.video_caption: Private beach. Outdoor pool. Three bars. Radisson Blu Resort on Bai Dai is living up to every bit of that 4.8/5 rating, and the sunset seals the deal.

tiktok.caption: Private beach + 3 bars + that sunset view at Radisson Blu, Phu Quoc. Bai Dai is the move. #PhuQuoc #BaiDai #VietnamTravel #BeachResort

scenes: Stay for the sunset. Trust us.
